mnemonics: FINAL stops the CHAIN: Classes can't be extended, Methods can't be overridden, Variables can't be changed.
keyPoints: A final class cannot be extended (inherited from).
A final method cannot be overridden by a subclass.
A final variable is a constant; its value cannot be changed once assigned.
commonMistakes: Trying to extend a class declared as 'final' (e.g., the String class).
Attempting to override a method declared as 'final' in the superclass.
Forgetting to initialize a final instance variable in its declaration or in every constructor.
